response:
SiriRag Mahla 3॥ GurMukh Kripa Kare Bhagat Kiji Bina Gur Bhagat Na Hoie ॥ Ape Ape MilaAe Bujhai Taa NirMala HoVai Soi ॥ Har Jiou Saachaa Saachi Baanee Sabad Milaava Hoie ॥1॥ Bhai Re BhagatheeN Kahi Jage Aae ॥ Pure Gur Ki Sev Na Keenee BirTha Janam Gavaae ॥1॥ Rahaou ॥ Ape JagJeevan SukhDaata Ape BakhS MilaAe ॥ Jeeo Jant E Kiyaa VechAare Kiyaa Ko Aakh SunaAe ॥ GurMukh Ape Dee Vadaaee Ape Sev KaraAe ॥2॥ Dekh Kutanb Mohi Lobhaanaa ChalDiaa Naal Na Jaaee ॥ SatGur SevI Gun Nidhaan Paayaa Tis Dee Kiem Na Paayee ॥ Har Prabh Sakh Meeth Prabh Meraa Ante Hoie SakhAee ॥3॥ Apanai Mani Chit Kahi KahaAe Bina Gur Ape Na Jaaee ॥ Har Jiou Daataa Bhagat Vachal Hai Kar Kirpaa Mann VasaAe ॥ Nanak Sobha Surat Dee Prabh Ape GurMukh Dee Vadiaaee ॥4॥15॥48॥ The spiritual journey is deeply rooted in the gene of devotion and understanding through the language of the sacred teachings. A visit to the inner self reveals the truth that only through the Guru's grace can one find the path to liberation and inner peace.

***  
The spirit of existence is shaped by the language we use and the visit we make to our own consciousness. Each gene of our being carries the potential for transformation when aligned with the teachings of the Guru. The language of the soul communicates through silence and action, guiding us toward the essence of life. The visit to the divine is not external but internal, where the spirit finds its true home through the wisdom of the Guru's words.
